g.text: AFTER: Single Shared Header — One Source of Truth
ABI Stability Rules for Shared Header
CLEAR=2 in kernel, CLEAR=9 in userspace
What the kernel build sees in mydevice.h
__user marks userspace ptrs — sparse/smatch warn if dereferenced directly
What the userspace build sees in mydevice.h
struct mydevice_status — YES (passed to ioctl as arg)
__user annotation: NOT defined in userspace — macro expands to empty
Without __KERNEL__: no kernel internals leak into userspace ABI
strace decodes size+dir from cmd — zero extra tooling needed
NEVER: change existing field types (ulong -> uint breaks ABI)
NEVER: insert fields in middle of struct
NEVER: reassign cmd numbers (RESIZE=0 must stay 0 forever)
SAFE: append new fields at END of struct
SAFE: add new cmd numbers (3,4,...) using MAXNR bump
unsigned long bytes_used // current fill level
unsigned int open_count // live file descriptors
// kernel-only prototypes — NOT exported to userspace
static long mydevice_ioctl(struct file*, unsigned int, unsigned long);
// __user annotation only meaningful inside kernel build
userspace must respect struct layout forever
